description: Machine session state. Provider-neutral: every agent (Claude, Codex, Gemini, or any other) and every human reads this before governed work. Update it at end of session and point active_handoff at the newest handoff.
next_allowed_move: XR1S-RECIPROCAL-WORKSPACE-LINK-2026-07-24 is now the active tranche: ORCHESTRATOR->SPEC_AUTHOR->WORK_ORDER_AUTHOR authored ADR-2026-07-24-XR1S-RECIPROCAL-WORKSPACE-LINK, SPEC-XR1S-RECIPROCAL-WORKSPACE-LINK-2026-07-24, and WO-XR1S-RECIPROCAL-WORKSPACE-LINK-2026-07-24, opening this repository's reciprocal authorization for CVF-Operations-Workspace's pushed XR1-O-C1 (Operations authorization commit 74170650bd7f2732bc2eec985e5b891df6d45897, post-push continuity 3ed0fc83cc542f9c2af2c17ee9cbed60b891e74a, Operations HEAD==origin/main==3ed0fc83..., Operations overallStatus XR1_O_C1_PUSHED_WAITING_SHIFT_AUTHORIZATION). Decides: relationship identity (workspaceId cvf-operations-workspace, Shift PROFILE_SOURCE, Operations PRIMARY_PLATFORM, direction SHIFT_TO_OPERATIONS_GOVERNED_INTAKE); a future five-field Shift descriptor with NO sourcePin (Operations exclusively owns its own accepted-Shift-commit pin); a mandatory split between XR1-S-C2a (one-line .cvf/manifest.json core-pin repair, 6ce1cf0->27137db4) and XR1-S-C2b (.cvf/workspace-link.json + one descriptor test), never combined; descriptor-test requirements; BUILD gates for each future commit; and a claim boundary that proves relationship identity and the core-pin repair only - not Operations' acceptance of any Shift commit, not the refresh tool's existence, not High Finding #4 closure, and not P2B-APPROVER-IDENTITY-RECONCILIATION's completion. REPAIR ROUND 1 (2026-07-24, REPAIR_WORKER): independent Codex review returned REVIEW_FAIL with three findings, all repaired without waiver. XR1S-R1 IMPOSSIBLE_FULL_DOCTOR_PASS - corrected every full-doctor-PASS requirement so the core/manifest row must reach [PASS] while the overall result may stay PASS WITH NOTE only with the exact pre-existing LEGACY_PROJECT: governed downstream catalog kit not present warning, never a new or different one. XR1S-R2 DETERMINISTIC_CATALOG_GATE_CONFLICT - XR1-S-C2b's ceiling gains docs/catalog/MODULE_REGISTRY.json and docs/catalog/MODULE_CATALOG.md as a conditionally-authorized ceiling (not a requirement), bounded to reconciling only the exact metric drift the two required files cause, via the canonical generate_catalog.py --write, never hand-edited; removed the prior mid-BUILD-stop/amendment language. XR1S-R3 UNNECESSARY_RECONCILER_SIDE_EFFECT - the hidden core is already clean and at the exact target commit, so XR1-S-C2a is now verify-only (no reconciler run, no _cvf-core-backups/ side effect); a drifted core at BUILD time stops for independent review instead of being silently reconciled. All prior decisions preserved: XR1-S-C2a/XR1-S-C2b remain separate commits; the Shift descriptor keeps exactly five fields with no sourcePin/pinUpdatePolicy; Operations remains the acceptance-pin owner; P2B-APPROVER-IDENTITY-RECONCILIATION remains parked. STATUS: XR1S_AUTHORIZATION_REPAIRED_PENDING_INDEPENDENT_RE_REVIEW - no BUILD occurred; .cvf/workspace-link.json was NOT created; .cvf/manifest.json was NOT touched this round. NEXT MOVE: Codex acts as independent REVIEWER performing a re-review of this repaired authorization package, confirming XR1S-R1/R2/R3 are each closed without waiver; only after REVIEW_PASS may XR1-S-C1 be staged/committed/rehearsed/pushed, and only then may XR1-S-C2a/XR1-S-C2b each separately be authorized to BUILD. THIS TRANCHE IS ORTHOGONAL TO THE LANE SEQUENCE, exactly as CVF-CORE-PIN-2026-07-23 previously was inserted without touching it: P1B-OPERATIONS-DOMAIN-EXTRACTION remains FREEZE/CLOSED_BOUNDED (C1 3e3df420bceca97d8047927a2098ea726d427aa8 -> C2 1e56a72e2259f142fd26cc3035e81814f2856d35 -> C2b ab75abb854607694740c0558d00a5d4a4cb99dfd -> C3 f68cf634f67984d64562dd8bfd0962ef01eb91fe -> C4 closure; full detail preserved verbatim in verification_snapshot.p1b_operations_domain_extraction, not repeated or lost here), and the operator's confirmed four-lane order (P1-B done; lane 2 known-principals.yaml<->users; lane 3 P2-A incidents/handovers; lane 4 P2-C) is NOT being reordered, cancelled, or advanced. Lane 2's own WORK_ORDER draft (P2B-APPROVER-IDENTITY-RECONCILIATION, docs/work_orders/P2B_APPROVER_IDENTITY_RECONCILIATION_WORK_ORDER.md, committed at this repository's current HEAD f98f29e145fa002be070e9d44520d20f0f82dcb3, Status: DRAFT - NOT APPROVED. BUILD IS NOT AUTHORIZED.) is explicitly PARKED by this insertion - not edited, resumed, superseded, cancelled, and its BUILD is not begun; it remains exactly where it was left, awaiting its own future operator-approved INTAKE resumption. Do not conflate XR1-S's closure with lane 2's resumption - they are independent tranches.
commit_discipline: Commit and push to origin main as soon as each roadmap tranche is complete and gates pass. Do not batch multiple tranches into one commit. Commit message must name the tranche id and verification result. Customer_request repair 0429c4a and bootstrap-continuity acc5d09 are the retained example of separate commits for separate batches. cd36b27 (P2-B) is retained as historical evidence of an unauthorized build candidate and must NOT be rewritten, squashed, amended, or force-pushed - corrective work lands in new commits only.
blocked_work: Rewriting or combining committed customer_request repair 0429c4a and bootstrap-continuity acc5d09; they are separate reviewed commits.

Treating CVF_SESSION/ACTIVE_SESSION_STATE.json as a canonical or independent source of truth - it is a compatibility mirror only, required by scripts/check_cvf_workspace_agent_enforcement.ps1's literal path check; SESSION/ACTIVE_SESSION_STATE.json is the sole canonical active state. Run python scripts/check_session_state.py (verify_mirror_drift) before finishing any session that edits either file.

Reusing the labels 'enforced', '12/12', or 'golden vertical' without the callable/load-bearing/not-verified-server-side distinction from docs/cvf/CVF_CONTROL_MAPPING.md (2026-07-22 correction) - this distinction is now permanent practice, not just a P-FIX-era caveat.

Claiming SqlLedger is production-verified on PostgreSQL without an actual PostgreSQL round-trip test run against a migration-created schema (the known static defect - missing tasks.version column - is fixed, but this has never been run against a live PostgreSQL instance; no Docker daemon is available in this environment). Live verification is a pre-ship gate, not required for ordinary SQLite-based development.
Claiming cost/termination are load-bearing before an AI mode beyond NO_AI is wired.

Claiming approval identity is fully authenticated - known-principals.yaml only rejects fabricated/inflated approver claims; it is not a signature/token/session check. P2-B (2026-07-22) shipped real authentication for get_principal/identity but deliberately did NOT replace or reconcile known-principals.yaml - that remains open (High Finding #4), not fixed by P2-B.
Claiming freeze is enforced for report_approved/open_handover_items_linked - those two prerequisites have no model yet and are only satisfied via an explicit, audited override, not a real check.

Trusting InMemoryLedger.get_event/get_task/get_shift to return a live reference in any NEW code - they return model_copy(); do not reintroduce direct dict access that bypasses transaction() semantics.

Adding a new domain vertical without wiring it through Ledger.transaction() for atomic mutation+audit, without a parent-shift-frozen check, and without persisting its evidence the way events/tasks do - these are now the established pattern, not optional extras.
Adding provider-named entrypoint files (CLAUDE.md as content, etc.) - front door must stay provider-neutral (CONTRIBUTING.md).
Batching more than one roadmap tranche into a single commit.

Claiming 'P-FIX CLOSED' or 'all Critical/High findings fixed' without a fresh probe/test run - a prior agent made exactly this overclaim after P-FIX-5 and a second independent review rejected it (P-FIX-6, 2026-07-22: anonymous shift.close bypassed governance entirely). Use 'P-FIX CLOSED_BOUNDED' and always re-verify before repeating a closure claim from a prior handoff.

Claiming High Finding #4 is fixed - it remains open after P2-B (2026-07-22): known-principals is still a registry check, not real authentication, and P2-B deliberately did not reconcile it with the new users table. Identity itself IS now fixed (P2-B) - do not lump it back in with this still-open finding.

Adding a new router endpoint that mutates a governed record without first checking whether an application service (ShiftService/TaskService/EventService/CorrectionService pattern) already exists for that record type and routing through it - the P-FIX-6 gap was exactly a router calling the ledger directly, bypassing the service that would have enforced identity/permission/audit.

Claiming 'P2-A closed' or 'P2-A done' without the customer_request/incidents/handovers split - only customer_request was replicated in the P2-A-CUSTOMER-REQUEST tranche (2026-07-22); incidents and handovers have no migration table yet and remain open. State precisely which domain(s) are done.

Implementing incidents or handovers by inventing a migration table ad hoc - both require a NEW governed migration first (unlike customer_request, which already had one), which is a bigger/riskier change than replicating the chain over an existing table and needs its own scoping.

Claiming P2-B shipped refresh tokens, revocation, self-service registration, password reset, or login rate-limiting - none of these were built; access tokens are valid for their full fixed TTL (JWT_ACCESS_TOKEN_TTL_MINUTES) with no early invalidation.

Treating scripts/seed_dev_users.py as a real user-provisioning flow - it is dev/test only, with fixed printed passwords; do not use it to provision real users, and do not claim a real admin-provisioning flow exists.

Reusing X-User-Id/X-User-Role headers as an identity mechanism anywhere in new code - get_principal (P2-B, 2026-07-22) only accepts a verified JWT bearer token; those headers now carry no authority at all, proven by tests/cvf/test_shift_close_governance.py::test_old_header_impersonation_no_longer_grants_any_identity.

Rewriting history to claim cd36b27 itself was authorized: it remains the original UNAUTHORIZED_BUILD_CANDIDATE. Governance approval belongs to the later P2B-AUTHENTICATION-REPAIR corrective chain, which reached FREEZE on 2026-07-23 without rewriting cd36b27.
Rewriting, squashing, amending, or force-pushing commit cd36b27 - it is retained as historical evidence of an unauthorized build candidate. Corrective work lands in new commits only.
Skipping DESIGN, SPEC, or WORK_ORDER for the P2B-AUTHENTICATION-REPAIR corrective tranche, or beginning BUILD before an operator explicitly approves the WORK_ORDER draft.

Treating a TestClient/local-probe review (as the prior 'independent review' was) as satisfying Mandatory Governance Proof (AGENTS.md) for a claim that CVF identity is load-bearing - that requires a real provider (Alibaba) API call, evidence recorded sanitized (no raw key/Authorization header), not a mock.
Using mock/TestClient output as future governance evidence. P2-B's approved claim is backed by the sanitized real-provider receipt; later governance claims need their own live evidence when required.
Expanding P2-B FREEZE into approval-quorum, PostgreSQL, refresh/revocation, admin-provisioning, or AI-gateway claims; each remains outside this tranche's verified boundary.

Claiming the CVF-CORE-PIN-2026-07-23 tranche demonstrates, verifies, or evidences AI/agent governance behavior - it synchronized a documentation-only CVF core delta and a manifest pin. Workspace doctor 24/24 proves local enforcement artifacts and public-core freshness only, and WORKSPACE_RULES.md states that boundary itself; it does not substitute for live provider-backed governance evidence.

Re-opening or re-litigating the P1-B lane drift - it was RESOLVED by the operator at INTAKE on 2026-07-23, who confirmed the order P1-B, then known-principals-to-users, then P2-A incidents/handovers, then P2-C. Do not re-rank the lanes, do not edit EXECUTION_ROADMAP.md to make the two surfaces agree retroactively, and do not declare either surface authoritative over the other. The historical record of the drift stays in next_allowed_move and in the CVF-CORE-PIN handoff; do not delete it.

Starting, scoping, or implementing lanes 3-4 (P2-A incidents/handovers, P2-C frontend) out of order. With P1-B (lane 1) now FROZEN, the operator's confirmed next lane is lane 2 - the known-principals.yaml to authenticated users reconciliation (High Finding #4) - and it starts at a fresh INTAKE. Do not skip ahead to lane 3 or 4, and do not start ANY lane from a loose chat instruction: each lane needs its own INTAKE and its own authorization artifacts.

Re-running, re-opening, or re-litigating any P1-B gate. P1B-OPERATIONS-DOMAIN-EXTRACTION reached FREEZE/CLOSED_BOUNDED on 2026-07-23 with C1 3e3df42, C2 1e56a72, C2b ab75abb, C3 f68cf63 (independent REVIEW_PASS AC-01..AC-18) and the C4 closure. Those commits are settled history; corrective work, if ever needed, lands in NEW commits, never a rewrite/amend/squash/force-push.

Editing, resuming, superseding, cancelling, or beginning BUILD on P2B-APPROVER-IDENTITY-RECONCILIATION (docs/decisions/ADR_2026-07-23_P2B_APPROVER_IDENTITY_RECONCILIATION.md, docs/specs/P2B_APPROVER_IDENTITY_RECONCILIATION_SPEC.md, docs/work_orders/P2B_APPROVER_IDENTITY_RECONCILIATION_WORK_ORDER.md, committed f98f29e145fa002be070e9d44520d20f0f82dcb3, Status: DRAFT - NOT APPROVED. BUILD IS NOT AUTHORIZED.) - it is explicitly PARKED by the XR1S-RECIPROCAL-WORKSPACE-LINK-2026-07-24 tranche, which is an orthogonal governance-core insertion, exactly as CVF-CORE-PIN-2026-07-23 previously was inserted without touching the lane sequence.

Creating .cvf/workspace-link.json during XR1-S-C1 - it is authorized only as an exact future shape (ADR-2026-07-24-XR1S-RECIPROCAL-WORKSPACE-LINK section 2.2) and is built, if ever, only at a separately-authorized XR1-S-C2b.
Editing .cvf/manifest.json during XR1-S-C1 - the 6ce1cf0/27137db4 drift is the XR1-S-C2a prerequisite, not permission to touch the manifest during this authoring round.
Combining XR1-S-C2a (core-pin repair) and XR1-S-C2b (reciprocal descriptor) into one commit, at any future point - they must remain independently committed, reviewed, and revertible.

Adding sourcePin, pinUpdatePolicy, or any other consumer-acceptance-state field to the future Shift reciprocal descriptor - Operations exclusively owns its own accepted-Shift-commit pin; Shift asserting it would be a category error (see ADR-2026-07-24-XR1S-RECIPROCAL-WORKSPACE-LINK section 2.2).

Writing to CVF-Operations-Workspace or the CVF core repository from this tranche - XR1-S only inspects CVF-Operations-Workspace read-only, to verify the two named authorization/continuity commit hashes.

Refactoring the SqlLedger(models=...) injection seam or the Ledger Protocol to import operations_domain directly. P1-B deliberately left packages/operations-ledger/** with a zero-line diff because operations_ledger._rows.build_user needs models.User, which stays in the injected workspace_api.domain.models namespace. Tightening the Protocol is a later, separately-authorized tranche.

Moving the User model into operations-domain - it did NOT move in P1-B. User belongs to the authentication boundary and its relocation is lane 2's decision, not a mechanical side effect. It stays defined in workspace_api/domain/models.py, which after the extraction is BOTH a compatibility shim (for the 12 operational types) and the canonical home of User.

Re-declaring or subclassing any of the 12 moved operational types or 3 lifecycle guards anywhere outside operations_domain - the shims must re-export the SAME objects (tests/unit/test_operations_domain_shim_identity.py asserts `is`, not `==`, per module pair). A second definition would create two classes that fail each other's isinstance checks and diverge in Pydantic schema output.

Claiming operations-domain is 'enforced', or anything beyond 'partial'. P1-B moved it stub->partial: the 12 types and 3 guards have a canonical home, but incidents, handovers, reports, approvals and audit still have NO model or runtime, and the per-domain blueprint subdirectories are still README-only. It becomes 'enforced' only when it fully owns a governed vertical end-to-end - not before.

Claiming the Phase 1 exit gate is met because P1-B is ticked - ticking a roadmap item is not a phase gate. The PostgreSQL live round-trip has still never been run in this environment and remains a pre-ship gate; its wording must survive verbatim.
Editing .cvf/manifest.json again for the core-pin tranche - the pin is already committed at da9a122. Any further manifest change needs its own authorization.

Regenerating docs/catalog/** or editing docs/implementation/EXECUTION_ROADMAP.md or docs/cvf/CVF_CONTROL_MAPPING.md as part of the core-pin FREEZE - no module status, roadmap item, or control status changed; generate_catalog.py --check passing without regeneration is the containment proof.

verification_snapshot.pytest: verify by running `python -m pytest -q` - do not trust a number written here, it goes stale every tranche
verification_snapshot.make_validate: PASS (catalog + session-state + file-size checks). catalog --check now recomputes metrics/Markdown and fails on drift (negative-tested).
verification_snapshot.cvf_controls_callable: 12/12 (see docs/cvf/CVF_CONTROL_MAPPING.md for the callable/load-bearing distinction). Identity is load-bearing and governance-approved within the bounded P2-B claim after corrective REVIEW_PASS and live Alibaba evidence PASS on 2026-07-23. Approval/known-principals High Finding #4 remains open and must not be conflated with identity.
verification_snapshot.p_fix_tranche: CLOSED_BOUNDED. P-FIX-1 freeze (cross-record, both backends), P-FIX-2 audit (atomic with mutation, both backends), P-FIX-3 evidence (persisted through SqlLedger) + approval (known-principal check, interim), P-FIX-4 migration Task.version + tightened schema parity, P-FIX-5 catalog --check (real metric/Markdown drift detection), P-FIX-6 shift.close governed (identity/permission/audit via ShiftService.close, closing the anonymous-close bypass a second independent review found after P-FIX-5's closure claim). 'Bounded' means: every Critical/High finding found by BOTH independent reviews to date has an end-to-end test; it does NOT mean no further gap can exist - do not restate this as 'all findings fixed'.
verification_snapshot.p2a_customer_request: DONE (2026-07-22, tranche P2-A-CUSTOMER-REQUEST). CustomerRequestService is the fifth service replicating the same cvf-runtime gate chain (identity/permission/domain_lock/audit for create - no risk/evidence/approval, since customer_requests has no risk_class/evidence column in the migration; identity/permission/lifecycle/audit for transition). customer_requests table verified two-directionally against the migration by the schema-parity suite. P2-A (incidents, handovers) remains OPEN - neither has a migration table yet.
verification_snapshot.p2b_authentication: FREEZE (2026-07-23). cd36b27 remains the original unauthorized build candidate. Corrective tranche P2B-AUTHENTICATION-REPAIR completed operator-approved INTAKE->DESIGN->SPEC->WORK_ORDER->BUILD, repaired 8 findings, received independent REVIEW_PASS, repaired the live script's regional endpoint after an honestly recorded HTTP 401, and then produced live Alibaba evidence PASS with qwen3.7-max HTTP 200. Identity is load-bearing and governance-approved within the receipt boundary. See SESSION/handoffs/AGENT_HANDOFF_2026-07-23_P2B_AUTHENTICATION_REPAIR_FREEZE.md.
verification_snapshot.known_remaining_defects: data minimization is advisory only; data_scope/cost/termination have no runtime caller yet (AI-gated); refusal routing/recording per refusal-policy.yaml not implemented; known-principals is a registry check, not real authentication, and P2-B did not reconcile it with the users table; PostgreSQL round-trip never run live (no Docker in this environment); P2-A incidents/handovers have no migration table yet; P2-B has no refresh token/revocation and no real admin user-provisioning flow (scripts/seed_dev_users.py is dev/test only).
verification_snapshot.second_independent_review: docs/decisions/EA_INDEPENDENT_REVIEW_2026-07-22_CODEX.md - read before trusting any 'enforced' or 'CLOSED' claim in this repo; its recommended fix order (freeze -> atomic audit -> evidence/approval -> migration/parity -> catalog) is fixed, plus the shift.close governance gap it also implied (freeze's shift_closed check trusts close_shift) is now closed by P-FIX-6.
verification_snapshot.third_review_reminder: A prior handoff (AGENT_HANDOFF_2026-07-22_PFIX5.md) claimed the P-FIX tranche fully closed and was wrong. Do not repeat that pattern: verify with a fresh probe/test run before restating any closure claim, even one recorded in this file.
verification_snapshot.portable_clone_continuity: VERIFIED on 2026-07-22 from GitHub project head 3aa8b680618e9ba31891c7eb0f1d11a5ed6caa79 with public CVF core pin c1076dc4be9ef9058b7c4e7b96def59c26aab148. Fresh clone initializer returned workspace doctor 24/24, FRESH_CLONE_CONTINUITY_PASS, active handoff exists, and ignored local binding. Superseded on 2026-07-23 by tranche CVF-CORE-PIN-2026-07-23: the pin is now 6ce1cf00c31a7f825d4c3fa3e66e8a3509e4a4b2 - see cvf_core_pin below.
verification_snapshot.p1b_operations_domain_extraction: FREEZE / CLOSED_BOUNDED (2026-07-23, tranche P1B-OPERATIONS-DOMAIN-EXTRACTION). The control chain ran in full - INTAKE -> DESIGN -> SPEC -> WORK_ORDER -> BUILD -> REVIEW -> FREEZE - with every gate a separate commit: C1 3e3df420bceca97d8047927a2098ea726d427aa8 (ADR + SPEC + WORK_ORDER, zero implementation files), C2 1e56a72e2259f142fd26cc3035e81814f2856d35 (pre-BUILD continuity recording the operator's lane choice), C2b ab75abb854607694740c0558d00a5d4a4cb99dfd (authorization amendment - ADR addendum + SPEC amendment 1 + WORK_ORDER amendment 1, zero implementation files - moving the two catalog paths from C4 into C3 after a catalog-gate conflict surfaced in BUILD), C3 f68cf634f67984d64562dd8bfd0962ef01eb91fe (BUILD, exactly 42 paths, independent REVIEW_PASS AC-01..AC-18), and this C4 closure (roadmap + IMPLEMENTATION_STATUS + continuity, committed separately, NO catalog path). WHAT WAS DONE: the 12 operational types (DataState, RiskClass, ShiftStatus, TaskStatus, CustomerRequestStatus, EvidenceRef, Shift, Message, OperationalEvent, Correction, Task, CustomerRequest) and 3 lifecycle guards (assert_transition, assert_task_transition, assert_customer_request_transition) now have a single canonical definition in operations_domain; workspace_api.domain.models/.lifecycle are compatibility shims that re-export the same objects with proven object identity (`is`, not `==`, per module pair). All moved-type imports were repointed to operations_domain (32 import lines changed); the documented User imports, the SqlLedger shim-namespace imports, and the shim-identity test's imports intentionally remain. The package is a stdlib+pydantic-only sink (no reverse import of workspace_api/operations_ledger/cvf_runtime). User did NOT move - it belongs to the authentication boundary and its relocation is lane 2's decision; workspace_api.domain.models is therefore both a shim and the canonical home of User. packages/operations-ledger/** has a ZERO-LINE diff: the SqlLedger(models=...) seam was deliberately not refactored. operations-domain went stub -> partial, NOT enforced (incidents/handovers/reports/approvals/audit still have no model; blueprint subdirectories are still README-only). REVIEW EVIDENCE (re-run by the independent reviewer): targeted 71 passed, integration+cvf 210 passed, full suite 292 passed (221 baseline + 71 new) with 0 failed/0 errors, validators PASS, doctor 24/24, git diff --check clean, operations-ledger zero-line diff, and the AC-18 revert rehearsal in a temporary worktree matched C3_PARENT ab75abb and the 221 baseline with cleanup PASS. THE CATALOG-GATE CONFLICT was caught correctly as a stop condition in BUILD (generate_catalog.py --check recomputes metrics from source and validate_repository.py calls it, so with docs/catalog/** prohibited in C3 the AC-12/AC-13 pair was unsatisfiable); the fix went through DESIGN->SPEC->WORK_ORDER as the C2b amendment before BUILD resumed - the control chain catching an authorization defect rather than absorbing it into a BUILD commit. BOUNDARY: no API contract, schema, migration, lifecycle-semantics, enum-value or CVF-gate change; no authentication/approval/known-principals change; Phase 1 exit gate NOT met (PostgreSQL live round-trip never run - pre-ship gate); High Finding #4 remains OPEN and was untouched; no provider call and no secret read, because the tranche asserts no AI-governance claim. Next lane: known-principals.yaml <-> authenticated users reconciliation (High Finding #4) at a fresh INTAKE.
verification_snapshot.cvf_core_pin: FREEZE / CLOSED_BOUNDED (2026-07-23, tranche CVF-CORE-PIN-2026-07-23). The workspace doctor had been failing 23/24 with 'CVF public core matches origin/main -> BEHIND_PUBLIC_REMOTE' (local c1076dc vs origin/main 6ce1cf0). The hidden sibling core was reconciled with the framework's own sanctioned reconciler (.Controlled-Vibe-Framework-CVF/scripts/update_cvf_workspace_public_core.ps1, -WorkspaceRoot only; -UpdateProjectManifests/-AllowPendingCoreBackup/-OverlaySourcePath all prohibited and unused) and .cvf/manifest.json's cvfCoreCommit was re-pinned to the full 40-character hash. Commits: authorization artifacts 76e7360 (ADR+SPEC+WORK_ORDER, zero implementation files), BUILD with independent REVIEW_PASS da9a122 (.cvf/manifest.json only, 1 insertion / 1 deletion), FREEZE authorization addendum 18d67d3. Verified end state: core HEAD = core origin/main = manifest cvfCoreCommit = 6ce1cf00c31a7f825d4c3fa3e66e8a3509e4a4b2, core worktree clean, workspace doctor PASS 24/24 with FRESH_CLONE_CONTINUITY_PASS. The upstream delta was a single commit (6ce1cf0 'docs: reconcile seven-step loop and provider claims') touching four CVF core documentation files only - no script, template, or workspace kit file. P2-B FREEZE was committed separately as 4e15ea4; da9a122 and 4e15ea4 share no path, which is the direct evidence the two tranches were never batched. BOUNDARY: this tranche makes no AI/agent governance behavior claim and required no provider call; it does not alter the P2-B disposition, does not close High Finding #4, and does not advance any roadmap item. See SESSION/handoffs/AGENT_HANDOFF_2026-07-23_CVF_CORE_PIN_FREEZE.md.
verification_snapshot.xr1s_reciprocal_workspace_link: AUTHORIZATION AUTHORED, PENDING INDEPENDENT REVIEW (2026-07-24, tranche XR1S-RECIPROCAL-WORKSPACE-LINK-2026-07-24, ORCHESTRATOR->SPEC_AUTHOR->WORK_ORDER_AUTHOR, Claude, provider-neutral role contract). CVF-Operations-Workspace pushed its own portable-link authorization (ADR-OW-006/OW-XR1-SPEC-001/OW-XR1-WO-001, commit 74170650bd7f2732bc2eec985e5b891df6d45897, post-push continuity 3ed0fc83cc542f9c2af2c17ee9cbed60b891e74a, Operations overallStatus XR1_O_C1_PUSHED_WAITING_SHIFT_AUTHORIZATION) naming this repository PROFILE_SOURCE and itself PRIMARY_PLATFORM; Operations' own XR1-O-C2 BUILD is blocked until this repository's XR1-S-C1..C3 closes. Verified this round: Shift HEAD=origin/main=f98f29e145fa002be070e9d44520d20f0f82dcb3, worktree clean except the untracked assessment file (sha256 168ea2c7a67a31bae50c9e4dbe78c2273a692f3a82a1074585e1bdb89b70fde2, unchanged), workspace doctor RESULT: PASS WITH NOTE (24 passed, 1 warning(s)) - core/manifest row FAIL warn-only plus one unrelated catalog-kit WARN, full suite 292 passed. No provider call was made and no secret was read. Claude does not self-grant REVIEW_PASS and did not stage, commit, or push any file. REPAIR ROUND 1 (2026-07-24, REPAIR_WORKER): independent Codex review returned REVIEW_FAIL with three findings, all repaired without waiver. XR1S-R1 IMPOSSIBLE_FULL_DOCTOR_PASS - the core/manifest row must reach [PASS], no new [FAIL]/[WARN] may appear, and the overall doctor result may stay PASS WITH NOTE only with the exact pre-existing LEGACY_PROJECT: governed downstream catalog kit not present warning. XR1S-R2 DETERMINISTIC_CATALOG_GATE_CONFLICT - XR1-S-C2b's ceiling now conditionally authorizes docs/catalog/MODULE_REGISTRY.json and docs/catalog/MODULE_CATALOG.md, a ceiling not a requirement, bounded to the exact metric drift the two required files cause, reconciled only via generate_catalog.py --write, never hand-edited. XR1S-R3 UNNECESSARY_RECONCILER_SIDE_EFFECT - the hidden core is already at the target commit and clean, so XR1-S-C2a is now verify-only (no reconciler run, no _cvf-core-backups/ side effect); a drifted core at BUILD time stops for independent review instead. Repaired exactly the eight paths this round's ceiling permits; no ninth path; no BUILD, stage, commit, or push occurred; .cvf/manifest.json and the Operations repository were not touched. Status: XR1S_AUTHORIZATION_REPAIRED_PENDING_INDEPENDENT_RE_REVIEW. Next governed move: Codex acts as independent REVIEWER performing a re-review of ADR-2026-07-24-XR1S-RECIPROCAL-WORKSPACE-LINK, SPEC-XR1S-RECIPROCAL-WORKSPACE-LINK-2026-07-24, and WO-XR1S-RECIPROCAL-WORKSPACE-LINK-2026-07-24 as repaired, confirming XR1S-R1/R2/R3 each closed without waiver.

cvf_bootstrap_continuity_contract.note: Fields below were added 2026-07-22 to satisfy the CVF Project Bootstrap Continuity Contract's machine-readable active-state requirements. See docs/CVF_BOOTSTRAP_LOG_2026-07-22.md for the full reconciliation record and AGENTS.md for why this project keeps SESSION/ instead of CVF_SESSION/.

cvf_bootstrap_continuity_contract.controlChainModelNote: This is the seven-step governance control chain from AGENTS.md, distinct from this project's own five-phase business roadmap referenced by 'roadmap' above (docs/implementation/EXECUTION_ROADMAP.md). The control chain governs how one unit of work moves through gates; the roadmap governs what is being built and in what order.
